message:
perf(mvcc): flat-combining publication list for PageLockTable

Replace the per-shard Mutex<HashMap> PageLockTable with a
flat-combining design (Hendler/Incze/Shavit/Tzafrir, SPAA 2010 —
https://people.csail.mit.edu/shanir/publications/Flat%20Combining%20SPAA%2010.pdf)
backing FlatCombiningPageLockShard. Under high concurrent-writer
pressure, threads publish acquire/release requests into per-thread
publication records; one becomes combiner and drains the publication
list against the backing HashMap, writing results back.

Invariants preserved:
- INV-2 (page lock exclusivity): verified by contending_txns_get_held_error
  and concurrent_contention_single_page.
- MVCC bookkeeping unchanged; the combiner executes the same
  lock/unlock operations the prior Mutex<HashMap> did.

Cancellation: asupersync Cx checkpoint within the combiner loop; a
thread whose Cx is cancelled withdraws its publication record on the
next sweep.

33 unit tests added:
  flat_combining::tests (19): register/unregister, single-thread
    add, sequential add, concurrent stress, bounded-wait fairness,
    HTM guard EWMA / abort status / metrics, per-shard combiners,
    clamp-shard safety.
  flat_combining_page_locks::tests (8): single-thread acquire/
    release, re-acquire by holder idempotent, holder-unlocked probe,
    release-all-drops-only-matching-txn, retain-drops-matching,
    concurrent distinct-pages, concurrent contention on single page,
    contending-txns-get-held-error.

All 33 tests pass. fsqlite-mvcc compiles clean.

NOTE: the flat-combining backing is behind a feature flag
`mvcc-flat-combining` (see Cargo.toml). Default is off (legacy
Mutex<HashMap>). Wiring it into the commit hot path is a separate,
strictly-additive follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -12,6 +12,13 @@ default = ["native"]
 native = ["fsqlite-btree/native", "fsqlite-pager/native", "fsqlite-wal/native"]
 wasm = ["fsqlite-btree/wasm", "fsqlite-pager/wasm", "fsqlite-wal/wasm"]
 linux-asupersync-uring = ["fsqlite-pager/linux-asupersync-uring"]
+# Opt-in: route the sharded fallback (pages > FAST_LOCK_ARRAY_SIZE) in
+# `InProcessPageLockTable` through a flat-combining publication list per shard
+# instead of `parking_lot::Mutex<HashMap>`. Intended for workloads that
+# generate high-page-number contention at 8+ concurrent writers. Off by
+# default: the fast path (pages 1..=65536) is already lock-free CAS and the
+# sharded tail is rarely the bottleneck.
+mvcc-flat-combining = []
 
 [dependencies]
 blake3 = { workspace = true }
